description: Pre-scientific and traditional models of the natural world

traditions.name: Greek Atomism
traditions.description: Matter consists of indivisible atoms in an infinite void; precursor to atomic theory

traditions.name: Aristotelian Physics
traditions.description: Four elements (earth, water, fire, air) plus aether; natural place and motion

traditions.name: Vedic / Hindu Cosmology
traditions.description: Pancha Bhuta (5 elements); Vaisheshika atomic theory; cyclic cosmology of Yugas

traditions.name: Chinese Five Elements (Wuxing)
traditions.description: Five phases (wood, fire, earth, metal, water) and their dynamic interactions

traditions.name: Islamic Optics and Mechanics
traditions.description: First scientific theory of light and vision; early mechanics

traditions.name: Egyptian Cosmology
traditions.description: Cosmological framework with primordial waters (Nun), creation forces, and celestial mechanics

traditions.name: Mayan Astronomy
traditions.description: Highly accurate astronomical observations, calendrical systems, Venus cycles
